page_intro: Over two decades, this work has been carried by remarkable individuals — doctoral researchers, postdocs, visiting scientists, students, and collaborators — who passed through the lab and left their imprint on the science. Scroll down, and the present gives way to memory.

eras.name: Rostock
eras.intro: The Extreme Photonics Labs at the University of Rostock, where the group builds the next generation of light-field synthesizers, pushes dynamic picoscopy toward valence-electron imaging, and explores quantum phase transitions at petahertz frequencies.

members.name: Eleftherios Goulielmakis
members.note: ERC Advanced Grant holder. Pioneer of light-field synthesis and laser picoscopy. Built and led attosecond research groups in Munich, and now Rostock.

members.name: Annika Machotzek
members.note: Keeping the scientific machine running — from procurement and travel to finances and coordination.

members.name: Dr. Santanu Mandal
members.note: Exploring extreme nonlinear dynamics in nanostructures and light-field-driven petahertz electronics.

members.name: Zekun Pi
members.note: Developing next-generation light-field synthesizers for sub-femtosecond waveform control.

members.name: Praful Kumar
members.note: Working on dynamic picoscopy and attosecond-resolved imaging of valence electron motion.

members.name: Cyril Benny
members.note: Investigating strong-field phenomena at the petahertz frontier.

members.name: Alexander Bernhard Enders
members.note: Building advanced experimental setups for ultrafast photonics research.

members.name: Jan Dittrich
members.note: Essential technical expertise supporting the experimental infrastructure of the lab.

eras.name: Rostock Alumni
eras.intro: Those who passed through the Rostock lab and have moved on to new chapters — carrying the science with them.
eras.divider_text: As you go deeper, the present gives way…

eras.name: Munich & Garching
eras.intro: The Max Planck Institute of Quantum Optics and the Centre of Attosecond Science (MPC-AS) — where light-field synthesis was born, the first synthesized light transients were created, and the first real-time movie of valence electron motion was captured. A decade of breakthrough attosecond science.

members.name: Adrian Wirth
members.note: First experimental realization of a multi-channel light-field synthesizer.

members.name: Mohammed Th. Hassan
members.note: Co-developed optical attosecond pulse generation and control techniques.

members.name: Tran Trung Luu
members.note: Pioneer of petahertz spectroscopy — measured the first attosecond-resolved electron dynamics in solids.

members.name: Antoine Moulet
members.note: Developed EUV-based spectroscopic techniques for tracking ultrafast electron dynamics.

members.name: Manish Garg
members.note: First real-space, real-time imaging of valence-electron motion — a landmark of dynamic picoscopy.

members.name: Harshit Lakhotia
members.note: Laser picoscopy of graphene — sub-ångström imaging via strong-field re-collision.

members.name: Olga Razskazovskaya
members.note: Expert on multilayer optics and broadband high-reflectance coatings for synthesizer mirrors.

members.name: Alexander Guggenmos
members.note: Designed and fabricated attosecond multilayer optics critical to EUV experiments.

eras.name: Vienna & Crete
eras.intro: The formative years — first at FORTH in Heraklion, building high-order harmonic sources and learning to tame ultrashort pulses, then in Vienna and Munich where the first direct measurements of light waves led to a new era in attosecond physics.
eras.divider_text: …into the years where it all began.

members.name: Dimitris Charalambidis
members.note: Mentored the earliest experiments in strong-field physics and high-harmonic generation that started the journey.

members.name: Andrius Baltuška
members.note: Pioneered CEP-stabilized few-cycle pulses — essential tools for the first attosecond measurements.

members.name: Reinhard Kienberger
members.note: Key figure in the first attosecond streaking experiments — measuring light in real time.

members.name: Matthias Uiberacker
members.note: Part of the team behind the landmark attosecond experiments.

constellation.title: The wider constellation
constellation.intro: Mentors, long-term collaborators, and co-travellers — the broader network of scientists whose paths have intersected with the group's work over the years.
